content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 23, 2004


Brad W. Constantine, CPA
Bartig, Basler & Ray
1520 Eureka Road, Suite 100
Roseville, CA 95661

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-04-051

Dear Mr. Constantine:

       This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

       You are asking if you are a consultant under the provisions of the Act requiring
you to complete the statement of economic interests (Form 700) under the conflict of
interest code for the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District. We
are declining to provide this advice.

       The determination that you met the criteria of a consultant under the Act was
made by the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District. Your recourse


_____________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-04-051
Page No. 2

is with that agency, followed by a request to the Napa County Board of Supervisors, the
district's code reviewing body.²

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
JET:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\04-051
Enclosure

___________________________

²When the Commission is not the code reviewing body for an agency's conflict of interest code,
an individual seeking an interpretation of the code or its application must first request a determination from
the agency and the code reviewing body for such conflict of interest code. (Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(B),
copy enclosed.)

sections.question: You are asking if you are a consultant under the provisions of the Act requiring you to complete the statement of economic interests (Form 700) under the conflict of interest code for the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District.
sections.conclusion: We are declining to provide this advice. The determination that you met the criteria of a consultant under the Act was made by the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District. Your recourse is with that agency, followed by a request to the Napa County Board of Supervisors, the district's code reviewing body.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the requestor qualifies as a consultant under the Political Reform Act and must file a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700) for the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice, stating that the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District has already made the determination regarding consultant status. The requestor's recourse is to appeal to that agency and then to the Napa County Board of Supervisors as the code reviewing body.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (W-04-051). Document contains clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The FPPC declines jurisdiction because the determination was made by the local agency, not the FPPC, and regulatory procedures require the requestor to exhaust local remedies first.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You are asking if you are a consultant under the provisions of the Act requiring you to complete the statement of economic interests (Form 700) under the conflict of interest code for the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District.

CONCLUSION: We are declining to provide this advice. The determination that you met the criteria of a consultant under the Act was made by the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District. Your recourse is with that agency, followed by a request to the Napa County Board of Supervisors, the district's code reviewing body.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to advise whether a CPA qualifies as a consultant requiring Form 700 filing under the Napa County Flood Control and Water Conservation District's conflict of interest code, directing the requestor instead to challenge the district's determination with the agency and the Board of Supervisors.
